editableNewDialogue.text: Can you sanity-check a loop idea: keep a value constant for 60 steps, then bump it, repeat?

Yes. A common pattern is an outer loop for each minute-block, an inner loop for the 60 steps within that minute, then apply the increment once after the inner loop finishes. If you store outputs, you can push (time, value) pairs each inner iteration.
If I already have arrays for time and value, do I still need nested loops?

Not strictly—nested loops are just a clear way to generate/fill those arrays. If the arrays already contain per-second samples, you can do a single pass. If the arrays are per-minute (100 entries) and you need per-second (6000 entries), nested loops (or one loop over total seconds) is usually simplest.
What would a single-loop version look like?

You can loop totalSeconds = 100*60. Let minuteIndex = t / 60 and secondInMinute = t % 60. Then value = start + 0.5 * minuteIndex (constant across the 60 seconds), and time could be t or minuteIndex depending on what you want to record.
Unrelated: I saw a headline about a city testing 4-day workweeks—does that affect productivity?

Studies and pilots often report productivity staying similar or improving, with reduced burnout and absenteeism, but results vary by role and how meetings/scheduling are handled. It’s not universal, but many trials show neutral-to-positive outcomes.
Also unrelated: is it true some lighthouses still use Fresnel lenses?

Yes. Many historic lighthouses preserved Fresnel lenses (sometimes still operational, sometimes as museum pieces). Modern aids to navigation often use LED optics, but Fresnel lenses remain notable for their efficiency and distinctive design.
Back to code: if I increment an array element inside the outer loop, does it actually represent the next minute?

Only if you’re carrying the value forward. If field_strength[i] is meant to be the value for minute i, you should set field_strength[i] based on i (e.g., base + 0.5*i). If you do field_strength[i] += 0.5 at the end of the same i iteration, that updated value won’t automatically apply to i+1 unless you copy it or compute from a running variable.
Random question: why do some recipes insist on resting dough?
Resting lets flour hydrate and gluten relax, which can make dough easier to handle and improve texture. It can also help fermentation effects develop in yeasted doughs.
If I’m grabbing the ROG for the G‑Sync stuff, does it matter that I’ll be driving it off a GeForce card anyway?

If you’re on an NVIDIA/GeForce GPU, G‑Sync (or G‑Sync Compatible over Adaptive‑Sync) is the main practical differentiator—so yes, that pairing is exactly where the ROG’s sync feature can matter most. Just make sure the connection is via DisplayPort and that Adaptive‑Sync is enabled in both the monitor OSD and NVIDIA Control Panel.

About that monitor question earlier—I actually don’t care much about gaming features. I’m mostly after accurate colors and a comfortable screen for office work, so I’d rather prioritize an IPS panel and ergonomics.

Got it—so your priority isn’t advanced gaming features; it’s color accuracy and office comfort (typically IPS + good stand adjustability). That means the “best” choice should be judged on panel quality, calibration, and ergonomics rather than gaming extras like sync/HDR.
